titleStmt.title: SII 38.298: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 298. (A.R. No. 298 of 1912.) ATTI, CHEYYAR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On the north wall, central shrine, Agastīśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Kṛishṇadēvarāya Year: Śaka 1447. Parthiva, Mīṉa ba. di. 3, Thursday, Chittirai = 1526 A.D. March 1.

summary.p: Records that a certain Chōḷīśuram-uḍaiyār, son of Tuvārāpati-uḍaiyār (a resident) of Paḍaivīḍu along with Āṉaiāṉḍār, an official, exerted himself to secure tax-free status to the land (owned by the temple). It is also stated that he spent his wealth and after personal efforts he accomplished this for the sake of Edirilichōḷīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyanār temple at Atti in Attipaṟṟu attached to Rājagambīran-malai at Marudarasa-paḍaivīḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

It is also recorded that inorder to appreciate his services, he was given the kāṇiāṭchi with the office of accountant of the above temple, to maintain the income and expenditure of the temple. He was also given three pieces of different types of land, and also a house to reside in and was also entitled to receive a share of the consecrated food from the temple and other privileges that were accrued to the office of the accountant.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śvastiśrīman mahaā maṇḍalecura metiṉi micura kaṇṭakaṭṭāri cāḷuva EmaRead emmaṇṭalamum koṇṭaruḷiya rācātirācaṉrācaparamecuran pāṣekku tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyarkaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭukuṭātāṉ pūṟuvapaccimateṣaṇa Uttira camutiṟātipati śrīvirapiṟatāpaThe letter ‘pa’ is engraved below the line.kiṣṇatevamahārāyar pratavirājayam paṇṇiyaruḷā niṉṟa cakāpam 1000 4 100 4 10 7 mel cellāniṉṟa pāttipacamveṟcarattu miṉanāyaṟṟu Aparapakṣattu tritiyaiyum prahasa[pa*ti]vāramum peṟṟa cittaṟā nakṣatrattu ceyaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟakoṭṭattu marutaracarpaṭaiviṭu rācakempiran malaikkaṭaittapaṟṟu Attipaṟṟil meṟpaṭiyūril Uṭaiyār Etirilicoḷicuramuṭaiya nayiṉār koyilpaṇṭārattu kaṭavatāṉa 1000 100 It appears that this portion was deliberately chisled off.Immāṭāpattiyam kaṅkāṇi Āṉaiāṇṭārum Emmilicaintu paṭaiviṭṭilirukkum tuvārapati Uṭaiyār makaṉ coḷicuramuṭaiyār yiṉayiṉār koyilukku kaṅkāṇi Āṇṭāruṭaṉekūṭi meyvarunti tiraviyamum nentu caṟuva
māṉiyam paṇṇikkuṭukkayil nāṅkaḷ Ivaṟkuppaṇṇi kuṭutta kāṇi Āṭci yintakkoyilukku koyilkaṇakkeḻuttu Oru campiṟuti coḷicuramuṭaiyāṟku kāṇiāṭci paṇṇikuṭuttom koyilukkuvarum cakalaātāyamum veccamuṇṭāṉatum kaṇṭu Ivare koyilkaṇakkum cantirātittavarvarai Uḷḷavum Eḻutivara kaṭavārākavu koyilkaṇakku Eḻutumyiṭattu civitattukkuRead citaittu the symbol kku stands for kku.yinnāyiṉār tevatāṉa Āṉa AttimalaEri
kiḻ mutal maṭai pāccal kavuṇṭaṉ tam paṟṟil viṭṭa nañcai kuḻi 5 100� puñcaiyil Āṟṟaṅkaraiyile Ellai Ācaṟutikku meṟku perumāḷ tiruviḷaiyāṭṭattukku kiḻakku kuḻi 4 100�malaiyaṭiyil kollai kuḻi 4 100 Āka kuḻi 1000 5 100� vaṭakku teruvil maṉai Oṉṟum maṉai puḻakkaṭaiyum kuḻi 1000 5 100�On adding 5 100(500)+ 4 100(400)+ 4 100(400) The total extent of kuḻi was 1,300. But in the record the total figure is given 1,500 kuḻi (4 1000 5 100)taṅkaḷ kai(y)ppaṟṟāka taṅkaḷiṭa Eriyiṭṭu Aṉupavittu koḷḷa kaṭavāraākavu koyil Oṭukku piṟacātam maṟṟum peṟum (peṟum) cuvantiramum Eṅkaḷ matikāri paṟṟakRead paṟṟikoṇṭu
varakkaṭavarākavu yippaṭi cammatittu kāṇiāṭci paṇṇikuṭuttom coḷicuramuṭaiyāṟku koyil tāṉattār māṭāpattiyam kaṅkāṇi Āṇṭārum §
§ māṭāpattiyam kaṅkāṇiāṇṭār Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 38.298 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
